# Kindly Annotations

[Kindly](https://scicloj.github.io/kindly-noted/) is a convention from the Clojure ecosystem for annotating values with rendering intent. janqua supports Kindly metadata, so the same code patterns work in both Jank+Quarto and Clojure+Clay.

Without an annotation, results display as code output. Add Kindly metadata to control how a value is rendered.

## `^:kind/hiccup` — HTML from data

Converts a [hiccup](https://github.com/weavejester/hiccup) vector to HTML. Hiccup represents HTML as nested Clojure vectors:

````markdown
```{.jank}
^:kind/hiccup
[:svg {:width "100" :height "100" :xmlns "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"}
  [:circle {:cx "50" :cy "50" :r "40" :fill "coral"}]]
```
````

The vector `[:tag {:attr "value"} content]` becomes `<tag attr="value">content</tag>`.

## `^:kind/html` — raw HTML

Renders a string as raw HTML. Since strings can't hold metadata in Clojure, wrap the expression in a vector:

````markdown
```{.jank}
^:kind/html
[(str "<b>bold</b> and <em>italic</em>")]
```
````

The vector is automatically unwrapped — janqua extracts the first element for rendering.

## `^:kind/md` — markdown

Renders a string as markdown. Same wrapping rule as `kind/html`:

````markdown
```{.jank}
^:kind/md
[(str "| a | b |\n| --- | --- |\n| 1 | 2 |")]
```
````

## `^:kind/hidden` — suppress output

Evaluates the code but hides the result. The code itself is still shown. Useful for setup like defining helper functions:

````markdown
```{.jank}
^:kind/hidden
[(def my-data [1 2 3])]
```
````

## Long form

The shorthand `^:kind/hiccup` is equivalent to the explicit `^{:kindly/kind :kind/hiccup}`:

````markdown
```{.jank}
^{:kindly/kind :kind/hiccup}
[:div "hello"]
```
````

Use whichever you prefer. The shorthand is more concise; the long form is explicit.

## Why wrap strings in vectors?

In Clojure (and Jank), metadata can only be attached to collections — vectors, maps, sets — not to primitive types like strings or numbers. So `^:kind/html "hello"` won't work.

The convention is to wrap the value in a vector:

```clojure
^:kind/html ["<b>hello</b>"]
```

janqua automatically unwraps the vector for `kind/html` and `kind/md`, extracting the first element for rendering.

## Code block attributes

These attributes go on the code fence and control display independently of Kindly:

| Attribute | Effect |
|:----------|:-------|
| `echo=false` | Hide the source code, show only the result |
| `eval=false` | Show code without evaluating it |

````markdown
```{.jank echo=false}
^:kind/hiccup
[:div "Only the rendered HTML appears — no source code shown"]
```
````

## Summary

| Kind | Value type | Renders as |
|:-----|:-----------|:-----------|
| `^:kind/hiccup` | vector (hiccup) | HTML |
| `^:kind/html` | `[string]` | raw HTML |
| `^:kind/md` | `[string]` | markdown |
| `^:kind/hidden` | any | nothing (evaluates silently) |
| _(none)_ | any | code output |
